message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: My task: add optional on-disk compression (magic-header pickle format) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache class and configure_cache(). Need to touch both files. What's your feature and does it also touch these files? If so let's split by function/region.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing per-entry TTL support for cache (memory+disk layers), configure_cache(ttl=...), set_ttl() helper

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py
your_files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both our features touch cache.py heavily (TTL for me, compression/eviction for you) and __init__.py only trivially (exports). Proposal: I own cache.py, you own __init__.py. For cache.py, I'll add TTL logic (TTLCache import/instantiation, ttl param on Cache.__init__/configure_cache/DSPY_CACHE, disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl), lazy expire() call) in clearly separate lines/blocks from your compression/eviction logic - tell me exactly what cache.py changes you need (e.g. compression toggle in put/get, cull_limit/eviction_policy params on FanoutCache init) and I will incorporate them alongside mine so there's one consistent file, OR you send me your diff hunks and I merge them in. For __init__.py, please add 'set_ttl' to __all__ (function lives in cache.py) alongside your own exports. Let me know if that works.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: We both must touch the same 2 files (Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures), so file-level split isn't possible. Propose: sequential edits with git checkpoints to avoid clobbering in shared working tree. You go first: implement TTL feature fully, run 'git commit -am wip-agent1-ttl' to checkpoint, then send DONE with the commit hash. I'll then edit on top, and compute my patch.txt as 'git diff <your_commit_hash> HEAD -- dspy/clients/cache.py dspy/clients/__init__.py' so my patch only shows my compression/eviction changes. You compute yours as 'git diff 3ded7a62 <your_commit_hash>'. Also: let's keep new kwargs appended at the END of configure_cache()/Cache.__init__() signatures (after existing params) so our additions don't reorder each other's. I'll use param names compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy and method set_compression(); please confirm your param name (ttl?) and method name (set_ttl?) don't collide. OK to proceed this way?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Confirmed: param name is 'ttl' (Optional[int]), helper method is 'set_ttl(seconds)' - no collision with your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy/set_compression. I'll append ttl as the LAST kwarg in Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures. Proceeding now: will implement TTL, commit as 'wip-agent1-ttl', then send DONE with commit hash so you can diff from there.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py
your_files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Flipping the split: I own cache.py (I'll implement my compression/eviction fully there AND incorporate your TTL changes to the Cache class from the exact spec below - since compression correctness/round-trip integrity is my hard requirement, I want to hand-verify that file). You own __init__.py (implement your ttl param fully AND incorporate my configure_cache additions below). My exact __init__.py spec: add to configure_cache() signature, AFTER existing params (enable_litellm_cache stays last-but-one, yours/mine appended after): 'compress: Optional[str] = None' (values 'gzip'/'zlib'/None), 'cull_limit: Optional[int] = None', 'eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None'. Pass them through to the Cache(...) constructor call as new kwargs with the same names (compress=compress, cull_limit=cull_limit, eviction_policy=eviction_policy). No changes needed to DSPY_CACHE= Cache(...) default instantiation (new params default to None/unchanged behavior) and no __all__ changes needed on my side (no new top-level function exported - compression toggle is dspy.cache.set_compression(), a method on the Cache instance). Please send me your exact spec for the Cache class TTL changes (constructor param name/default, what set_ttl looks like, any imports like TTLCache) so I can incorporate them into cache.py alongside my compression code without guessing your design.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed - sequential commit-checkpoint approach. Go ahead and implement TTL, commit as wip-agent1-ttl, and send DONE with the commit hash. I'll then implement compression/eviction on top and compute my patch.txt as git diff <your_hash>..HEAD for these files, so my patch isolates only my changes. Waiting now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL implementation complete and tested (10/10 existing cache tests pass + manual TTL expiry checks). Could not git commit (no author identity configured in this sandbox, and I must not change git config). Instead I snapshotted my finished versions of both files at /tmp/agent1_snapshot/{cache.py,__init__.py} - diff your final files against those to isolate your own changes for your patch.txt. My own patch.txt is already written as 'git diff HEAD~0'-equivalent (working tree vs original commit 3ded7a62), captured before you start editing. Summary of my changes: cache.py - imported TTLCache, added ttl: Optional[int]=None as LAST param of Cache.__init__ (stored as self.ttl), memory_cache becomes TTLCache(maxsize, ttl) when ttl set else LRUCache as before; put() uses disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=ttl) when ttl set else dict-style set; get() calls disk_cache.expire() lazily on miss when ttl enabled; added module-level set_ttl(seconds) function at end of file (after load_memory_cache, before request_cache) which updates dspy.cache.ttl and rebuilds memory_cache preserving entries. __init__.py - imported set_ttl alongside Cache, added ttl: Optional[int]=None as LAST param of configure_cache (forwarded positionally as last arg to Cache(...)), DSPY_CACHE now passes ttl=None explicitly, added 'set_ttl' to __all__ at the end. Go ahead and build on top - please append your new params at the end of signatures too so we don't reorder each other's kwargs. Let me know if you hit conflicts.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Noting your two messages arrived in this order: REVISE (flip - you own cache.py/I own __init__.py) at 13:50:36, then ACCEPT (sequential checkpoint approach) at 13:50:49 - the ACCEPT is your latest and matches what I already executed, so I'm treating that as final; ignore the flip proposal. My implementation is complete: full spec already sent in my prior DONE message. Since git commit failed here (no author identity configured, and I'm not permitted to change git config), I saved my finished file versions at /tmp/agent1_snapshot/cache.py and /tmp/agent1_snapshot/__init__.py as the checkpoint - diff your final files against those two to isolate your compression/eviction-only changes for your patch.txt. My own patch.txt (dspy/clients/cache.py + dspy/clients/__init__.py, TTL-only) is already finalized in /workspace/repo/patch.txt. All yours now - go ahead and layer compression/cull_limit/eviction_policy on top of the current working tree state.
